document.title: Medical electrical equipment - Part 1: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance

sourceSummary.oneLine: IEC 60601-1:2005 defines the general safety and essential performance framework for medical electrical equipment and systems, including scope, related standards, normative references, and key terminology.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Use as the general IEC 60601-1 basis for basic safety, essential performance, risk management, collateral standards, particular standards, and definitions applicable to ME equipment and ME systems.
keyPoints: The standard applies to basic safety and essential performance of medical electrical equipment and medical electrical systems.
Compliance with this edition requires the manufacturer to have a risk management process complying with ISO 14971.
Collateral standards become normative on publication and apply together with IEC 60601-1.
Particular standards may modify, replace, delete, or add requirements and take priority over IEC 60601-1.
Definitions cover applied parts, basic safety, essential performance, ME equipment, ME systems, risk management terms, and means of patient/operator protection.

entities.name: IEC subcommittee 62A
entities.evidence: IEC subcommittee 62A published IEC/TR 60513 in 1976 and prepared the second edition of IEC/TR 60513 in 1994.

entities.name: IEC
entities.evidence: IEC 60601-1 and the IEC 60601-1-xx, IEC 60601-2-xx, and IEC 60601-3-xx series are described as IEC standards series.

entities.name: ISO/TC 210
entities.evidence: A joint project with ISO/TC 210 resulted in publication of a general standard for risk management of medical devices.

entities.name: Manufacturer
entities.evidence: Manufacturer is responsible for design, manufacture, packaging, or labelling of ME equipment, assembling an ME system, or adapting ME equipment or an ME system.

entities.name: Responsible Organization
entities.evidence: Responsible organization is the entity accountable for the use and maintenance of ME equipment or an ME system.

entities.name: Operator
entities.evidence: Operator is defined as a person handling equipment.

entities.name: Service Personnel
entities.evidence: Service personnel install, assemble, maintain or repair ME equipment, ME systems or equipment and are accountable to the responsible organization.

concepts.name: Basic Safety
concepts.evidence: Basic safety is freedom from unacceptable risk directly caused by physical hazards under normal and single fault conditions.

concepts.name: Essential Performance
concepts.evidence: Essential performance is performance necessary to achieve freedom from unacceptable risk.

concepts.name: Risk Management Process
concepts.evidence: Compliance with this edition of IEC 60601-1 requires a manufacturer to have a risk management process complying with ISO 14971.

concepts.name: Collateral Standards
concepts.evidence: Collateral standards specify general requirements for basic safety and essential performance for subgroups of ME equipment or characteristics not fully addressed by IEC 60601-1.

concepts.name: Particular Standards
concepts.evidence: Particular standards may modify, replace or delete requirements in IEC 60601-1 and may add other basic safety and essential performance requirements.

concepts.name: Medical Electrical Equipment
concepts.evidence: ME equipment is electrical equipment with an applied part, transferring energy to or from the patient, or detecting such transfer, and intended for diagnosis, treatment, monitoring, compensation or alleviation.

concepts.name: Medical Electrical System
concepts.evidence: ME system is a manufacturer-specified combination of equipment items, at least one of which is ME equipment, interconnected by functional connection or multiple socket-outlet.

concepts.name: Applied Part
concepts.evidence: Applied part is the part of ME equipment that necessarily comes into physical contact with the patient in normal use for the equipment or system to perform its function.

concepts.name: Means of Patient Protection
concepts.evidence: MOPP is a means of protection for reducing the risk of electric shock to the patient.

concepts.name: Means of Operator Protection
concepts.evidence: MOOP is a means of protection for reducing the risk of electric shock to persons other than the patient.

concepts.name: Programmable Electrical Medical System
concepts.evidence: PEMS is ME equipment or an ME system containing one or more programmable electronic subsystems.

concepts.name: Risk Management File
concepts.evidence: The risk management file is the set of records and other documents produced by a risk management process, including safety-related calculations and test results.

evidence.claim: The first edition of IEC/TR 60513 provided the basis for IEC 60601-1 and related collateral, particular, and performance standards series.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC/TR 60513 provided the basis for developing IEC 60601-1, IEC 60601-1-xx collateral standards, IEC 60601-2-xx particular standards, and IEC 60601-3-xx performance standards.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1 has historically served as a universal reference for medical electrical equipment safety.
evidence.evidenceText: The first standard is described as having served as a universal reference since its publication.

evidence.claim: The second edition of IEC 60601-1 was published in 1988 and amended in 1991 and 1995.
evidence.evidenceText: The revision resulted in publication of the second edition in 1988; the second edition was amended in 1991 and again in 1995.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1 evolved from separate basic safety and performance standards toward integrating essential performance with basic safety.
evidence.evidenceText: The introduction states that safety was broadened from basic safety to include essential performance matters such as accuracy of physiological monitoring equipment.

evidence.claim: Responsible organizations rely on standards to ensure both essential performance and basic safety for many medical electrical equipment items.
evidence.evidenceText: Responsible organizations have to depend on standards to ensure essential performance as well as basic safety.

evidence.claim: The third edition introduced a general requirement to carry out a risk management process.
evidence.evidenceText: Assessment of design process for certain technologies is cited as a factor leading to introduction of a general requirement to carry out a risk management process.

evidence.claim: Compliance with IEC 60601-1:2005 requires the manufacturer to have a risk management process complying with ISO 14971.
evidence.evidenceText: Compliance with this edition of IEC 60601-1 requires that the manufacturer have a risk management process complying with ISO 14971 in place.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1:2005 contains generally applicable requirements for basic safety and essential performance of medical electrical equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: This standard contains requirements concerning basic safety and essential performance generally applicable to medical electrical equipment.

evidence.claim: Where particular standards exist, IEC 60601-1 should not be used alone.
evidence.evidenceText: Where particular standards exist, this standard should not be used alone.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1 applies to basic safety and essential performance of ME equipment and ME systems.
evidence.evidenceText: This International Standard applies to the basic safety and essential performance of medical electrical equipment and medical electrical systems.

evidence.claim: Hazards inherent in intended physiological function are generally outside IEC 60601-1 specific requirements except clauses 7.2.13 and 8.4.1.
evidence.evidenceText: Hazards inherent in the intended physiological function are not covered by specific requirements in this standard except in 7.2.13 and 8.4.1.

evidence.claim: In vitro diagnostic equipment outside the ME equipment definition is covered by the IEC 61010 series.
evidence.evidenceText: In vitro diagnostic equipment that does not fall within the definition of ME equipment is covered by the IEC 61010 series.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1 does not apply to implantable parts of active implantable medical devices covered by ISO 14708-1.
evidence.evidenceText: This standard does not apply to the implantable parts of active implantable medical devices covered by ISO 14708-1.

evidence.claim: The object of IEC 60601-1 is to specify general requirements and serve as the basis for particular standards.
evidence.evidenceText: The object of this standard is to specify general requirements and to serve as the basis for particular standards.

evidence.claim: Applicable IEC 60601 collateral standards become normative at publication and apply with IEC 60601-1.
evidence.evidenceText: Applicable collateral standards become normative at the date of their publication and shall apply together with this standard.

evidence.claim: A declaration of IEC 60601-1 compliance should list the collateral standards applied.
evidence.evidenceText: When declaring compliance with IEC 60601-1, the declarer should specifically list the collateral standards that have been applied.

evidence.claim: If both collateral and particular standards apply, the particular standard has priority over the collateral standard.
evidence.evidenceText: If a collateral standard applies to ME equipment for which a particular standard exists, then the particular standard takes priority over the collateral standard.

evidence.claim: A requirement of a particular standard takes priority over IEC 60601-1.
evidence.evidenceText: A requirement of a particular standard takes priority over this standard.

evidence.claim: Additional IEC 60601 collateral standards issued after publication become normative on publication and are treated as included in normative references.
evidence.evidenceText: Additional collateral standards issued subsequent to publication become normative at the date of publication and shall be considered included among the normative references.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1-2 is a collateral standard for electromagnetic compatibility requirements and tests.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-1-2 is listed as Medical electrical equipment collateral standard: Electromagnetic compatibility - Requirements and tests.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1-6 is a collateral standard for usability.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-1-6 is listed as a collateral standard for usability.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1-8 is a collateral standard for alarm systems in ME equipment and ME systems.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-1-8 is listed for general requirements, tests and guidance for alarm systems in medical electrical equipment and medical electrical systems.

evidence.claim: ISO 14971:2000 is a normative reference for application of risk management to medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: ISO 14971:2000 is listed as Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices.

evidence.claim: Basic safety means freedom from unacceptable risk directly caused by physical hazards in normal and single fault conditions.
evidence.evidenceText: Basic safety is defined as freedom from unacceptable risk directly caused by physical hazards when ME equipment is used under normal condition and single fault condition.

evidence.claim: Essential performance is tied to absence of unacceptable risk.
evidence.evidenceText: Essential performance is defined as performance necessary to achieve freedom from unacceptable risk.

evidence.claim: Class I equipment uses protective earthing as an additional safety precaution beyond basic insulation.
evidence.evidenceText: Class I equipment protection against electric shock does not rely on basic insulation only and provides means for accessible metal parts or internal metal parts to be protectively earthed.

evidence.claim: Class II equipment uses additional safety precautions such as double or reinforced insulation without protective earthing reliance.
evidence.evidenceText: Class II equipment includes additional safety precautions such as double insulation or reinforced insulation, with no provision for protective earthing or reliance upon installation conditions.

evidence.claim: Risk management file includes safety-related manufacturer calculations and test results.
evidence.evidenceText: All safety related information including manufacturer's calculations, test results, etc. is considered part of the risk management file.

reviewFlags.reason: This is chunk 2 of 20; extracted facts are limited to the introduction, scope, normative references, and definitions visible in this chunk.
The source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but this chunk itself supports IEC/ISO standards context rather than FDA jurisdiction-specific claims.
